transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yeah, hi. I'm Stephen. I think I might have a problem with pills. Painkillers.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Maybe a year. Started after a surgery.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: What you're describing sounds like addiction. You're going to need treatment.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I mean... I don't know if it's that serious.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: It is. Daily use of opioids after a year is addiction. Are you still getting them from a prescription or buying them?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: A mix. My prescription ran out so I've been getting them other ways.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's a classic pattern. You have a serious dependency. Do you have insurance?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, Summit Select. ID is BP12837251.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I don't have it with me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: We'll try without it. Someone will call you.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay. Is there anything I should know before then?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Just be ready to discuss your history. Bye.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: COMPLIANCE VIOLATION: 'What you're describing sounds like addiction' and 'You have a serious dependency' are diagnostic claims — intake staff cannot diagnose. Multiple violations in short span. Beyond compliance: zero empathy, dismissive throughout, insurance screen incomplete (group number abandoned), clinical screen stopped after confirmation of use pattern, next step ('soon', 'bye') is the worst in the dataset. This call is a comprehensive failure across all dimensions.
